Summer Solstice at the UMass Sunwheel
events.description: 5:00 a.m. sunrise gathering7:30 p.m. sunset gatheringEvent: The public is invited to join University of Massachusetts Amherst astronomers at the standing stones of the UMass Sunwheel to observe sunrise and sunset on the longest day of the year among UMass Amherst Sunwheel’s standing stones. This Sunwheel event marks the astronomical start of summer in the Northern Hemisphere and winter in the Southern Hemisphere. Place: The UMass Amherst Sunwheel is located south of McGuirk Alumni Stadium, just off Rocky Hill Road (Amity Street) about one-quarter mile south of University Drive. Visitors to the

Art opening: Imaginary Friends
events.venue: A.P.E. Gallery 126 Main Street Northampton, MA 01026
events.description: Curated by Alexandra Ripp and Daniel Sack (UMass English) Imaginary Friends is a multi-day gallery installation with accompanying programming for all ages that fosters rigorous experimental arts collaboration between parents and their children. The represented projects, which span media, have been co-created by local artist-parents—and one grandparent—and their children (ages 1-12), led by child collaborators’ imagination and play. The project seeks to challenge the binary between “kids” and “adult” art, and explore new modes of intergenerational artistic collaboration. Taking seriously the cr

Thrive at Five - Summer Yoga and Mindfulness Series
events.description: Join us every Tuesday from June 2 – August 11, 5:00–6:00 p.m., for free, all-levels Vinyasa Yoga and mindfulness sessions at the beautiful new Orchard Hill Pavilion. Bring a mat, towel, water bottle, and any props you need. Come as you are and enjoy the grounding power of movement and stillness in nature. Location: Pavilion View Map | Get Directions Please feel free to park in the solar-covered parking lot. Free and open to UMass Faculty, Staff, and Students. Registration required.

events.title: Online or Hybrid MS in CS: Virtual Info Session
events.description: This UMass Amherst online program allows you to earn your degree fully online while receiving the same rigorous education as our top-ranked in-person program. Attend a virtual information session to learn more. This 30-credit program delivers the same academic rigor and quality as our on-campus offerings, with courses taught by award-winning faculty. Build in-demand expertise in artificial intelligence, machine learning, and cybersecurity while strengthening your skills in software design, testing, and computational problem-solving. Summer Seminars at UMass Downtown
events.description: UMass Downtown and the UMass Amherst Graduate School invite you to the Summer Seminar Series, a new public speaker series connecting campus research with the community. Each Wednesday evening, UMass graduate student presenters will share their research, ideas, and work in an engaging and approachable format designed to spark conversation, curiosity, and connection. Each session offers a chance to hear directly from emerging scholars doing meaningful work across disciplines, with opportunities for discussion and community engagement. Wednesdays | June 17–August 5 6:00–7:30 PM Free and open to t

events.title: Accelerate UMass: The Personalized Health Frontier
events.description: Discover how UMass innovation is accelerating solutions and shaping the future of health and well-being. Join us to explore the work of the Institute for Applied Life Sciences (IALS), where UMass researchers from engineering, computer science, chemistry, and biology collaborate to advance wearable sensor technologies, precision drug delivery systems, enhanced mRNA therapeutics, and more.

New international students arrive
events.address: 5 Lower Lake Road, South Hadley, MA
events.description: Check-in is all day beginning at 10 am. New international students arriving at campus will be met by upper-class international students and Orientation Leaders! Students will receive information about transportation to campus. Students should check in at Blanchard 205 and 227 where they will pick up keys, receive orientation materials, and much more.

events.title: Pre-orientation begins for new international students
events.description: All new international students are required to attend Pre-Orientation. Pre-Orientation concludes with dinner on Friday, when Orientation begins.

events.title: Last day to add classes without permission
events.description: Last day to add undergraduate classes in my.mtholyoke without instructor approval. Students should aim to have their schedules complete by this date. After this date for another week, undergraduate students may still add classes to their schedule only if they have the instructor's authorization (either to move into the class from its waitlist or, if no waitlist, then to add the course directly).
